story.iWant: Jest configuration ve test setup tamamlanmış olsun
story.soThat: unit, integration ve E2E testler yazabileyim ve test-driven development (TDD) yapabileyim

subtasks.subtask: Read existing package.json jest config (Epic 8.5-4'ten devralındı)
Remove jest config from package.json (move to jest.config.js)
Test configuration: npm test should discover and run tests
Create test/ directory if not exists
Ensure moduleNameMapper for path aliases exists
Test configuration: npm run test:e2e should work (even if no e2e tests yet)
Verify package.json test scripts (already exist from package.json)
Test all scripts: run each script and verify no syntax errors
Run npm run test:cov to generate coverage reports
Verify console shows coverage summary (text reporter)
Test coverage threshold enforcement (intentionally lower coverage → should fail)
Add coverage/ to .gitignore if not exists
Verify git status doesn't show coverage/ folder
Run npm test and verify execution completes
Review test output for any configuration warnings
Document any failing tests (expected if no tests written yet)
Verify moduleNameMapper in jest.config.js matches tsconfig paths
Verify moduleNameMapper in test/jest-e2e.json matches tsconfig paths
Create sample test using @/ import to verify resolution
Run test and verify no module resolution errors
Document test scripts usage in README (optional)
Run full test suite: npm test && npm run test:e2e && npm run test:cov

criterion.title: Jest Configuration File Oluştur (jest.config.js)
criterion.then: - moduleFileExtensions: ['js', 'json', 'ts'] - rootDir: '.' - testRegex: .*\\.(spec|integration-spec)\\.ts$ - testPathIgnorePatterns: ['/node_modules/', '/dist/'] - transform: ts-jest for TypeScript - collectCoverageFrom: ['src/**/*.(t|j)s'] - coverageDirectory: 'coverage' - testEnvironment: 'node' - moduleNameMapper: '^@/(.*)$': '<rootDir>/src/$1' (path alias support) - coverageThresholds: * global: { branches: 70, functions: 70, lines: 70, statements: 70 } * './src/common/**/*.ts': { branches: 100, functions: 100, lines: 100, statements: 100 } * './src/**/services/**/*.ts': { branches: 80, functions: 80, lines: 80, statements: 80 }

criterion.title: E2E Test Configuration Oluştur (test/jest-e2e.json)
criterion.then: - moduleFileExtensions: ['js', 'json', 'ts'] - rootDir: '.' - testRegex: '.e2e-spec.ts$' - transform: ts-jest for TypeScript - testEnvironment: 'node' - moduleNameMapper: path aliases (same as unit test config)

criterion.title: Package.json Test Scripts Ekle
criterion.then: - npm test: Tüm unit testleri çalıştır - npm run test:watch: Watch mode'da test çalıştır (TDD için) - npm run test:cov: Coverage report ile test çalıştır - npm run test:e2e: E2E testleri çalıştır

criterion.title: Coverage Reports Konfigürasyonu
criterion.then: - coverage/ klasörü oluşturulmalı - Console'a coverage summary yazdırılmalı (text reporter) - HTML report oluşturulmalı (coverage/index.html) - LCOV format report oluşturulmalı (coverage/lcov.info) - CI/CD için - Coverage thresholds enforce edilmeli (< 70% global → fail)

criterion.title: Gitignore Coverage Folder
criterion.then: - coverage/ satırı .gitignore'da bulunmalı - Git status'ta coverage/ klasörü görünmemeli - Coverage reports commit edilmemeli

criterion.title: Sample Test Çalışıyor
criterion.then: - Mevcut test dosyaları (src/app.controller.spec.ts) bulunmalı ve çalışmalı - Test execution başarılı olmalı (pass veya fail, syntax error yok) - Test discovery çalışmalı (*.spec.ts dosyaları bulunmalı) - TypeScript compilation çalışmalı (ts-jest)

criterion.title: Module Path Aliases Çalışıyor
criterion.then: - Jest path aliases'ı resolve etmeli - Import errors olmamalı - Test dosyaları "import { X } from '@/common/...'" syntax'ı kullanabilmeli

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Epic 9 provides comprehensive test infrastructure setup using Jest v30.x and @nestjs/testing. Coverage thresholds: Global 70%, Utilities 100%, Services 80%. Test pyramid: Unit → Integration → E2E. Based on hrsync-backend proven patterns.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Test pyramid implementation with folder structure: test/ for E2E configs and factories, src/modules/[module]/__test__/ for unit and integration tests. Coverage reports in coverage/ directory.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: jest.config.js → ts-jest (TypeScript compilation) → Test Files (*.spec.ts, *.integration-spec.ts). Test execution flow: Jest loads config, ts-jest compiles TypeScript, tests execute with TestingModule for unit tests or real DB for integration.

doc.title: PRD - Non-Functional Requirements: Coding Standards
doc.snippet: Test folder naming: __test__ or __tests__. Test file naming: [module-name].service.spec.ts, [module-name].integration-spec.ts, [name].e2e-spec.ts. All files use kebab-case.

doc.title: Epic 9: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Story 9.1 configures Jest with jest.config.js including moduleFileExtensions, testRegex for spec and integration-spec files, coverage thresholds, and path aliases. Package.json test scripts: test, test:watch, test:cov, test:e2e.

doc.title: Story 8.5-4: Test Fixes
doc.snippet: Epic 8.5-4 created test utilities in test/utils/: mockI18nService.ts, mockLogger.ts, mockPrismaService.ts, responseHelpers.ts. Added moduleNameMapper to both package.json jest config and test/jest-e2e.json. 513 tests passing.

artifact.reason: Current Jest config in package.json to be migrated to jest.config.js. Already has moduleFileExtensions, rootDir, testRegex, transform (ts-jest), collectCoverageFrom, coverageDirectory, testEnvironment, moduleNameMapper for path aliases.
Existing E2E config with moduleNameMapper, setupFiles, globalSetup/Teardown. Has testRegex for .e2e-spec.ts files. Needs verification for completeness.
TypeScript path aliases definition: "@/*": ["src/*"]. Jest moduleNameMapper must match this configuration.
Example of test utility pattern created in Epic 8.5-4. Factory function pattern for creating mocks. Use as reference for understanding existing test infrastructure.
Comprehensive Prisma mock with all models. Example of mock factory pattern. Shows how existing test utilities are structured.
Example unit test using @nestjs/testing TestingModule pattern. Shows Arrange-Act-Assert pattern, mock injection, and beforeEach setup. Should work after Jest config migration.
E2E test setup file referenced in jest-e2e.json setupFiles. Part of existing test infrastructure.
Global setup for E2E tests, runs once before all tests.
Global teardown for E2E tests, runs once after all tests.

packages.package: HTTP assertion library for E2E tests

constraint.title: Jest Config Extraction from package.json
constraint.description: Current Jest config is in package.json lines 103-126. Must extract to jest.config.js while preserving all settings. After migration, remove jest block from package.json.

constraint.title: Coverage Thresholds Must Be Enforced
constraint.description: Coverage thresholds defined in Tech Spec: Global 70%, Common utilities 100%, Services 80%. Thresholds must fail build if not met. Use coverageThreshold config.

constraint.title: Path Aliases Must Match tsconfig.json
constraint.description: moduleNameMapper in jest.config.js and test/jest-e2e.json must match tsconfig.json paths: "@/*": "src/*". Currently package.json has correct mapping.

constraint.title: Test File Naming Conventions (NFR-4.1)
constraint.description: Unit tests: *.spec.ts, Integration tests: *.integration-spec.ts, E2E tests: *.e2e-spec.ts. testRegex must match these patterns.

constraint.title: Test Environment Must Be Node
constraint.description: Backend API tests require testEnvironment: 'node'. No jsdom or browser environment.

constraint.title: Coverage Reporters: text, html, lcov
constraint.description: Must generate three report formats: text (console output), html (coverage/index.html), lcov (coverage/lcov.info for CI/CD).

constraint.title: Preserve Existing Test Infrastructure
constraint.description: Do not modify test/utils/* files created in Epic 8.5-4. Preserve jest-e2e.json setupFiles, globalSetup, globalTeardown references.

constraint.title: Coverage Folder Must Be Gitignored
constraint.description: coverage/ directory must be added to .gitignore to prevent committing test coverage reports.

interface.name: jest.config.js exports
interface.signature: module.exports = { moduleFileExtensions, rootDir, testRegex, transform, collectCoverageFrom, coverageDirectory, testEnvironment, moduleNameMapper, coverageThreshold, coverageReporters, testPathIgnorePatterns }

interface.name: Coverage Threshold Configuration
interface.signature: coverageThreshold: { global: { branches: 70, functions: 70, lines: 70, statements: 70 }, './src/common/**/*.ts': { branches: 100, functions: 100, lines: 100, statements: 100 }, './src/**/services/**/*.ts': { branches: 80, functions: 80, lines: 80, statements: 80 } }

tests.standards: Jest v30.x with ts-jest v29.2.5 for TypeScript compilation. Unit tests use @nestjs/testing TestingModule for dependency injection. Test pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA). Mock utilities in test/utils/: createMockI18nService(), createMockPrismaService(), createMockLogger(). Coverage thresholds: Global 70%, Common 100%, Services 80%. Coverage reporters: text (console), html (coverage/index.html), lcov (coverage/lcov.info). Test environment: node (no jsdom). Path aliases via moduleNameMapper matching tsconfig.json. Test naming: *.spec.ts (unit), *.integration-spec.ts (integration), *.e2e-spec.ts (E2E).

locations.location: src/*.spec.ts - Root level unit tests (e.g., app.controller.spec.ts)
test/utils/ - Shared test utilities and mocks

ideas.idea: Create jest.config.js and verify jest discovers existing tests correctly
Test coverage threshold enforcement by temporarily modifying thresholds
Verify jest-e2e.json configuration with npm run test:e2e
Run each npm test script individually to verify functionality
Generate coverage reports and inspect all three formats (text, html, lcov)
After adding coverage/ to .gitignore, verify git status excludes it
Run npm test to verify sample tests execute without errors
Create temporary test file with @/ import to verify path alias resolution
